title: nvm_read: doc says Ok(vec![]) for offset>=len but actually returns Err
description:
In nvm_read, when offset >= meta.len, saturating_sub yields data_len=0 and the call proceeds to the server. The server's _HandleNvmRead checks 'if (offset >= meta.len) return WH_ERROR_BADARGS' (wh_server_nvm.c:67-68) and rejects the request with an error code. The Rust caller therefore receives Err, not Ok(vec![]).

The docstring at line 160 says: 'Returns an empty Vec when offset >= meta.len.' This is a false contract.

Fix: add an early return 'if data_len == 0 { return Ok(vec![]); }' before the FFI call. This also avoids a round-trip to the server for a trivially-known-empty result.

Note: zero-length data_len with a dangling-but-non-null Vec pointer is technically safe because the server rejects before writing, but the documented/actual behavior mismatch is the primary problem.
